reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict was based solely on build.sh, which is an AppImage build script unused during makepkg execution. PKGBUILD and install scripts show no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged an unused build.sh script as a supply-chain risk, while Deepseek correctly focused on relevant build files.
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to review critical PKGBUILD build functions (prepare/build/package) despite their execution during makepkg
Both models skipped source tarball inspection (v1.22.1.tar.gz)
false_positives: build.sh (Qwen): AppImage script unused in PKGBUILD build process; normal dev tooling
learnings: Prioritize PKGBUILD functions (prepare/build/package) and .install scripts over unused dev/CI files
Improve model context: Emphasize that only makepkg-executed code is relevant
Add checksum verification for skipped binary files like source tarballs
Reduce false positives by validating file execution context before flagging risks
